title: Neural Scaling & Emergence: Chinchilla, Grokking & the Δd=2 Lens

chinchilla.why_eml2: Power law = exp(−α·log N): each term = EML-2 by log-integral equivalence

optimal_compute.rule: N* ∝ C^{0.5}, D* ∝ C^{0.5} (equal parameter and token budget)

kaplan.unified_claim: ALL neural scaling laws are EML-2: power law = log-log = exp+log paired. This is the universal fingerprint of Δd=2 (Direction B theorem). The true generalization process (EML-∞) has an EML-2 shadow: the scaling law.

scaling_laws.key_insight: All neural scaling laws = EML-2: power law = Horizon shadow of true learning dynamics

below_threshold.description: Loss follows smooth power law; capabilities absent or EML-0 level

at_threshold.type: TYPE 2 Horizon crossing (EML-2 → EML-∞ via phase transition)

above_threshold.description: New capability has its own EML depth
above_threshold.example: Chain-of-thought = EML-2 (reasoning chain = Markov process); formal proof = EML-3 (symbolic oscillatory)

is_emergence_controlled_d2.answer: NO — emergence is TYPE 2 Horizon, not a controlled Δd=2 inversion
is_emergence_controlled_d2.reason: Δd=2 inversion (Fourier, E[·]) is SMOOTH and predictable. Emergence is DISCONTINUOUS: no warning from scaling law. The sharp transition = Horizon crossing, not an inversion. Exception: if emergence is predicted by loss curvature changes (EML-2 precursor), then it may be a soft Horizon where EML-2 precedes the EML-∞ jump.

memorization_phase.description: Network memorizes training set via exponential memorization map
memorization_phase.loss: Training loss → 0, test loss stays high
memorization_phase.why_eml1: Memorization = lookup table = exp(−loss·scale): EML-1 (no log normalization)

grokking_transition.type: TYPE 1 finite depth change: EML-1 → EML-2
grokking_transition.description: Sudden generalization: model finds the true distribution (adds log-normalization)
grokking_transition.why_d1: Adding the normalization/log step = one primitive added = Δd=+1

generalization_phase.loss: Both train and test loss low
generalization_phase.why_eml2: Generalization = statistical estimation = exp+log paired = EML-2

grokking.eml_insight: Grokking = delayed EML-1 → EML-2 transition. The delay = the time it takes the network to find the log-normalization partner. Weight decay accelerates grokking because it penalizes the EML-1 memorization regime, forcing the network to find the EML-2 solution faster. This is a TYPE 1 controlled Δd=+1 transition, NOT a Horizon crossing.

classical_u_curve.description: Bias-variance tradeoff: EML-2 (variance = integration over noise)

interpolation_threshold.description: At N=n (parameters=data points): benign overfitting regime begins
interpolation_threshold.type: TYPE 2 Horizon: smooth EML-2 → EML-∞ interpolation

second_descent.description: Beyond interpolation: loss decreases again to EML-2 regime

emergence_grokking.key_insight: Three distinct transitions in neural networks: (1) Emergence: TYPE 2 Horizon (EML-2→EML-∞, discontinuous). (2) Grokking: TYPE 1 Δd=+1 (EML-1→EML-2, controlled). (3) Double descent: EML-2→EML-∞→EML-2 (Horizon crossing then return).

key_theorem: The Δd=2 Lens Applied to Neural Scaling (S240): ALL neural scaling laws (Kaplan, Chinchilla) are EML-2: power laws = exp(−α·log N) = log-integral equivalence (Direction B). Emergence thresholds are NOT controlled Δd=2 inversions — they are TYPE 2 Horizon crossings: discontinuous jumps from EML-2 (scaling law regime) to EML-∞ (capability phase transition). Grokking IS a controlled transition: TYPE 1 Δd=+1 (EML-1 memorization → EML-2 generalization). The key distinction: Emergence = TYPE 2 Horizon (discontinuous, unpredictable from EML-2 scaling). Grokking = TYPE 1 finite (controlled, predictable from the EML-1/EML-2 boundary). Double descent: EML-2 → EML-∞ → EML-2 (Horizon crossed and returned).
rabbit_hole_log: All scaling laws = EML-2 power laws: universal Horizon shadow of true learning dynamics
Emergence = TYPE 2 Horizon crossing (EML-2→EML-∞): NOT a controlled Δd=2 inversion
Grokking = TYPE 1 Δd=+1 (EML-1 memorization → EML-2 generalization): delayed but controlled
Double descent: EML-2 → EML-∞ → EML-2 (brief Horizon excursion)
